D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Axial Chromatic Aberration

watch a simple lens focus blue and red light at different points along the axis

Because the index n rises toward blue, a lens is stronger for blue than for red. Its focal length therefore varies with wavelength, smearing a point into a coloured streak along the axis.

Set the green focal length and choose the glass dispersion (Abbe-like). Blue rays focus short, red rays long; the gold span marks the axial colour error between the F and C lines.
